items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Seasonal Weakness Dominates Despite Expanding LNG Export Capacity
items.summary: Physical natural gas prices fell Monday as weak demand expectations and a sharp drop in May futures, in their first session as the front-month, weighed on markets nationwide.

items.title: Futures Settle: Spring Fundamentals Undercut Iran Risk Premium as May Natural Gas Rolls Into Lead
items.summary: May Nymex natural gas futures rolled into the front-month position Monday with a whimper, settling down 13.8 cents at $2.887/MMBtu as soft shoulder season fundamentals outweighed lingering support from Iran war-driven energy market volatility.

items.title: Cyclone Cuts Australian LNG Production, Tightening Supply Outlook and Pushing Prices Higher
items.summary: Global natural gas prices swung higher on Monday after another day of mixed signals over the Middle East war and a cyclone disrupted operations at several export terminals in Western Australia.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: U.S. Natural Gas Spot Prices Retreat on Mild Weather, Ample Storage
items.summary: Natural gas spot prices fell across the United States through midday on Monday as robust supply and mild weather sent bulls packing.

items.title: Mild Weather, Tepid Demand Likely to Lift Lower 48 Natural Gas Storage Cushion
items.summary: NGI is projecting the latest U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) Weekly Natural Gas Storage Report for the week ended March 27 shows an injection of 38 Bcf.

items.title: Weekly Natural Gas Spot Prices Slide as Mother Nature Takes Spring Break
items.summary: Weekly natural gas cash prices gave up ground amid mild temperatures and light heating demand across much of the Lower 48.

items.title: MidDay Futures: Natural Gas Slumps as Robust Supply, Mild Forecasts Offset Geopolitical Risk
items.summary: U.S natural gas futures fell in early trading Monday as record-level production and warm weather outlooks outweighed tensions in the Middle East and the rising cost of crude oil.

items.title: Golden Pass LNG Hits First Production, Adding Demand to Gulf Coast Natural Gas Price Hubs
items.summary: Golden Pass LNG has achieved first LNG at the Texas export terminal, setting the stage for more Gulf Coast feed gas demand and additional LNG supply in the coming weeks.

items.title: Midmorning Markets: Technical Barriers Contain Natural Gas Futures — For Now
items.summary: Natural gas futures kicked off the week trading in a range dictated in part by technical resistance, though war in the Middle East threatened to inject volatility at any point — as it has for the past month.

items.title: Golden Triangle Natural Gas Storage Gets Green Light to Expand to 60 Bcf-Plus
items.summary: FERC has cleared the way for what could become the Gulf Coast’s largest natural gas storage hub, granting a certificate for Golden Triangle Storage LLC to add 30 Bcf of capacity at its salt dome complex in Beaumont, TX.

items.title: Nigeria Pays 65% More for Gasoline as Dangote Battles Crude Import Costs
items.summary: Despite the fact that Nigeria is now home to the biggest oil refinery in Africa, Nigeria’s gasoline prices have soared to a record high amid the war in the Middle East. Dangote, the biggest refinery in Africa which began operations in 2024, has alrea

items.title: Oil Spike Collides With Fragile Global Growth
items.summary: The worst energy crisis in history is sending ripples across global markets, and optimism is in short supply. IEA’s chief said the current crisis is worse than all previous ones put together. JP Morgan analysts warned Asia is going to be hit the hard

items.title: QatarEnergy Brings U.S. LNG Online Amid Ras Laffan Fallout
items.summary: Golden Pass LNG, a joint venture between QatarEnergy and ExxonMobil (NYSE:XOM), has achieved first production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) at its first liquefaction train, marking the transition of the facility from a receiving terminal into a majo
